mnemonics: C A R G S: Count, Array, Return, Garbage, Start - The main function's common arguments and purpose.
keyPoints: The main function is the entry point of a C program.
Its standard signature is 'int main(int argc, char *argv[])' where argc is the argument count and argv is the argument vector.
It returns an integer exit status to the operating system (0 typically indicates success).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to return an integer value, leading to undefined exit status.
Assuming argv[0] is the first user argument instead of the program name.
Not checking the value of argc before accessing argv elements, potentially causing a segmentation fault.
